description:
What: fz-q9g.6 (D1: case-arm join into shared dst) was scoped against the pre-pattern-matrix case lowering. Main's PR #14 replaces bespoke case lowering with a unified pattern matrix compiler (src/pattern_matrix.rs +897, src/ir_lower.rs +1129). The notion of 'N case arms reach one join continuation, each allocating their own tuple' may have been restructured by the matrix compiler into a decision tree of If/Let with specialized leaves.

This ticket is investigation-only. Output: a written note attached here that either:
  (a) confirms the join-cont shape survives in the new lowering — fz-q9g.6 proceeds as written; or
  (b) describes the new shape and rewrites fz-q9g.6's plan (target node, fixture, success criterion) accordingly.

Approach:
1. Lower fixtures/case_tuple_pattern_sequential and fixtures/wildcard_then_specific to IR after rebase.
2. Inspect: do multiple arms still call a shared cont with HeapKind-typed result, or has the matrix compiler specialized arms with no shared cont?
3. Locate the alloc_struct / alloc_tuple call(s) per arm in the post-matrix CLIF.
4. Decide whether 'share one caller-allocated dst across arms' is still a valid rewrite.

Why: 'No hidden surprises. All investigation required must be done up-front.' fz-q9g.6 cannot start until this lands.

Removes: nothing (pure investigation).

Tests: investigation note; no code changes.

comments.text:
Investigation complete.

D1 (fz-q9g.6) target shape PRESERVED. src/ir_lower.rs:2885 lower_case under the matrix arc still mints a per-case join cont (case_join) in non-tail position. Each clause body lives in its own cont fn (case_clause_<i>_cont), and all clause bodies end with finalize_arm(result, join_opt) which tail-calls the shared join cont with the result. N arms reach one join cont; each may allocate the result independently. D1's optimization (give caller a dst, all arms write into dst) still applies.

Concrete witness: case_tuple_pattern_sequential's g_s17 has block2 (matched arm, extracts x) and block3 (else, returns 0) both tail-calling sig0 (the same cont). That IS the D1 shape.

Side-effect of matrix arc that HELPS the arc: combined with main's effective_returns LFP, the matrix narrows more spec keys to monomorphic return Descrs (it collapses arms that would have widened the join). More specs will classify to a single HeapKind under C1, so more specs become Dst-eligible. C1's harvest grew.

INTERACTION FLAGGED on C4 (fz-q9g.5): is_pure_tail_caller (PR #14) eats the small-K hop before C4 would fire. C4 still applies for above-budget / multi-callsite / closure-referenced K. Fixture choice matters — noted in fz-q9g.5 body.

DECISION: D1 proceeds as written. C1, C2, C3, D2, F-arc, B-arc, G-arc, E-arc: no changes from this investigation.

title: fz-dps.D1.0: re-investigate case-arm join under pattern matrix
